mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates above 5% compress equity discount rates and increase corporate borrowing costs, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand across durable and non-durable sectors, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization that triggers short-term positive equity momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum fails to generate positive 5-day returns in >60% of observations during tightening periods, or rolling IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent consumer price index levels above 305 combined with federal funds rates exceeding 5% create a restrictive financial environment that suppresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered observations, or the joint threshold condition shows no statistical edge (Rank IC < 0.02) over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the labor force and supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the signal's predictive accuracy (Rank IC) falls below 0.03 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized expansion in real GDP and industrial production confirms broad-based economic strength that reduces recession probability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and INDPRO fails to yield positive 5-day returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production alongside rising unemployment signal a supply-side contraction that dampens corporate output expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal generates positive 20-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the joint predictive power (Rank IC) remains below 0.02 during late-cycle phases.
